artifacts.title: Diarrhoea and vomiting
assertions: Adults should seek urgent advice for inability to keep fluids down, bloody diarrhoea or rectal bleeding, persistent diarrhoea over seven days, vomiting over two days or ongoing dehydration.

Vomiting blood or coffee-ground material, green vomit in adults, suspected poisoning, sudden severe abdominal pain, severe breathing difficulty, confusion or abnormal responsiveness requires emergency assessment.
Older age, weakened immunity and other conditions can increase dehydration risk.
Most uncomplicated episodes are short-lived and infection control includes handwashing and staying away from work until 48 hours after the last diarrhoea or vomiting episode.

artifacts.title: Dehydration
assertions: Reduced or dark urine, dry mouth, thirst, dizziness on standing and worsening alertness can indicate dehydration.
Vomiting and diarrhoea cause loss of fluid and electrolytes; oral rehydration may be appropriate when emergency features are absent.

artifacts.title: Food Poisoning Symptoms
assertions: Severe foodborne illness features include bloody diarrhoea, diarrhoea over three days, high fever, frequent vomiting preventing fluid intake and dehydration.
Pregnancy, age 65 or older and weakened immunity increase risk of severe foodborne illness.
Food exposure, illness contacts and symptom timing inform assessment but do not establish an organism.

artifacts.title: Suspected cancer: recognition and referral — nausea, vomiting and diarrhoea
assertions: Persistent unexplained nausea, vomiting or diarrhoea with weight loss, upper abdominal pain, back pain or age-dependent features can require investigation consideration.
The interview records persistence and weight loss without assigning cancer.

artifacts.title: STOM SNOMED CT lookup and MRCM allowed attributes for vomiting and diarrhea
assertions: STOM confirmed active 422400008 Vomiting (disorder) and 62315008 Diarrhea (finding).
Finding site and Severity were allowed for both focus concepts.
MRCM validates terminology modeling only and does not determine clinical questions, priority, diagnosis or safety.
